skill.name: cross-disciplinary-bridge-finder
skill.description: Analyzes conceptual connections between scientific disciplines to identify collaboration opportunities, translate methodologies across domains, and generate interdisciplinary research proposals.
skill.summary: Maps knowledge bridges between scientific fields and generates actionable cross-disciplinary collaboration proposals.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 143 static analysis findings evaluated as false positives. The skill is a legitimate research analysis tool that builds conceptual knowledge graphs between academic disciplines. Weak cryptographic algorithm findings triggered on domain descriptions containing terms like 'encryption' and 'key' but no actual cryptography is implemented. Shell backtick findings are markdown code formatting, not code execution. The skill instructs Claude Code to execute its Python script (external_commands) and save results to user-specified files (filesystem) which are expected behaviors for a CLI research tool.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm in domain description data
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static scanner matched on cryptographic terminology ('encryption', 'key', 'hash', 'protocol') in Domain dataclass keyword lists and descriptions for the 'cryptography' academic discipline. The code defines 20 academic domains as a knowledge graph and does NOT implement any cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance via standard library usage
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Scanner flagged 'import sys' and 'datetime.now()' as system reconnaissance. These are standard Python library imports used for argument parsing and output timestamping in a CLI analysis tool.

low_findings.title: Ruby shell backtick execution in markdown files
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Scanner interpreted markdown code formatting backticks as Ruby shell backtick execution operators. SKILL.md and audit-reference.md use standard markdown backtick syntax for code blocks and inline code formatting.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Python script execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Skill documentation instructs AI to execute 'python scripts/main.py' with CLI arguments. The script accepts source/target fields, depth, and output file parameters. While the code is benign, execution of external scripts is a risk factor that should be monitored.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem write operation
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes analysis output to a user-specified file path via the --save CLI argument. File path originates from user-controlled command-line input. The write operation itself is expected behavior for a report generator.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded external reference URL
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL to the skill's source repository on GitHub. No active network requests are made by the code, but the documentation URL represents a potential reference to external content.

content.value_statement: Researchers often work in isolated domains and miss valuable opportunities for cross-disciplinary collaboration. This skill maps conceptual connections between scientific fields to generate bridge paths, analogies, and actionable innovation proposals.
actual_capabilities: Maps conceptual connections and analogies across academic disciplines using a built-in knowledge graph
Discovers bridge paths linking distant scientific fields with scored novelty and feasibility
Generates innovation hypotheses at the intersection of two or more disciplines
Produces structured collaboration proposals with rationale, timeline, and expertise requirements
Analyzes multi-domain landscapes for comprehensive cross-disciplinary insight
Outputs results in both structured JSON and readable Markdown formats
limitations: Domain knowledge is limited to 20 predefined academic fields and their relationships
Does not access live databases, publication repositories, or real researcher profiles
Conceptual similarity calculations use keyword overlap, not deep semantic understanding of research content
Generated innovation hypotheses follow templates and require expert validation before real-world use

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Development
use_cases.description: A research administrator identifies novel cross-disciplinary themes by mapping bridge connections between traditionally separate fields, creating a foundation for large interdisciplinary grant applications.

use_cases.title: Interdisciplinary Research Team Formation
use_cases.description: A principal investigator maps complementary expertise across departments by analyzing bridge scores and conceptual mappings, enabling data-driven team assembly for a new research center.

use_cases.title: Interdisciplinary Curriculum Design
use_cases.description: A curriculum developer finds natural conceptual connections between course topics across departments and uses bridge analogies to design integrated interdisciplinary programs.

prompt_templates.title: Discover Bridges Between Two Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Find conceptual bridges between [field A] and [field B]. Include the strongest bridge paths, key analogies between core concepts, and specific innovation opportunities at their intersection.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher wants to discover connections between two unrelated disciplines to spark new ideas.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Interdisciplinary Research Hypotheses
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate research hypotheses at the intersection of [field A] and [field B]. Cover methodology transfer, analogical insights, hybrid approaches, problem reframing, and tool adaptation. Include required expertise and timeline for each hypothesis.
prompt_templates.scenario: A research group is brainstorming novel directions that combine expertise from two departments.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Domain Landscape Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the full collaboration landscape across these domains: [list of 3 or more fields]. For each pair, find the best bridge path and rank all connections by bridge score. Highlight the most promising and most novel pairings.
prompt_templates.scenario: A funding agency wants to identify the most promising cross-disciplinary clusters across multiple research areas.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Formal Collaboration Proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a detailed collaboration proposal connecting [field A] expertise with [field B] methods to address the research question: [question]. Include required team composition, timeline with milestones, key challenges, and measurable success criteria.
prompt_templates.scenario: A team lead is preparing a formal research collaboration proposal for institutional review board approval.

output_examples.input: Find bridges between machine learning and immunology
output_examples.output:
Bridge Analysis: Machine Learning x Immunology

Path: Machine Learning -> Optimization -> Complex Systems -> Immunology

Bridge Score: 0.723

Key Analogies:
- Generalization -> Immune Memory: Both systems learn from past encounters to improve future responses
- Gradient Descent -> Clonal Selection: Iterative improvement through selective pressure

Innovation Opportunity:
Apply attention-based deep learning architectures to model antibody-antigen binding kinetics for vaccine design.

output_examples.input: Generate hypotheses for neuroscience and computer science collaboration
output_examples.output:
Hypothesis 1: Neuromorphic Computing for Attention Mechanisms
Impact: High | Timeline: 2-4 years
Rationale: Strong conceptual bridge between neural coding in the brain and self-attention in transformer models.

Hypothesis 2: Reframing Memory Consolidation as a Data Compression Problem
Impact: Medium | Timeline: 3-5 years
Rationale: Both fields model information retention and retrieval under capacity constraints.

Required Expertise: neuroscience, machine learning, hardware design, information theory

output_examples.input: Analyze landscape across physics, biology, and economics
output_examples.output:
Multi-Domain Bridge Analysis:

Top Pair: Physics x Biology (Score: 0.850)
Path: Physics -> Complex Systems -> Biology
Opportunity: Apply statistical mechanics to model cellular decision-making.

Second Pair: Biology x Economics (Score: 0.750)
Path: Biology -> Complex Systems -> Economics
Opportunity: Use evolutionary game theory to study market behavior as an ecosystem.

Third Pair: Physics x Economics (Score: 0.720)
Path: Physics -> Mathematics -> Economics
Opportunity: Transfer renormalization group methods from physics to macroeconomic analysis.

best_practices: Start with a clear research question and specific field names before running bridge analysis to get the most relevant paths.
Validate generated hypotheses and analogies with domain experts before incorporating them into grant proposals or publications.
Combine multiple bridge paths between the same field pair to build a comprehensive narrative for collaboration proposals.
anti_patterns: Treating generated bridge scores as definitive measures of research quality without expert review of the underlying conceptual mapping.
Using the skill output to make claims about real researcher expertise or existing collaborations without independent verification.
Relying solely on template-based hypotheses for competitive grant submissions without substantial customization and expert refinement.

faq.question: What domains are included in the knowledge graph?
faq.answer: The knowledge graph includes 20 academic domains such as biology, computer science, physics, neuroscience, economics, and mathematics.

faq.question: Can I add custom domains to the analysis?
faq.answer: The current version uses a predefined set of 20 domains. You can extend the Python source code to add more domains and edges.

faq.question: How are bridge scores calculated?
faq.answer: Bridge scores combine path strength, novelty, and feasibility using configurable weights with defaults of 0.5, 0.3, and 0.2 respectively.

faq.question: Is this tool suitable for real grant applications?
faq.answer: It provides a starting point for brainstorming. All proposed hypotheses and collaborations need expert review before submission.

faq.question: Does this skill access external databases or APIs?
faq.answer: No. All analysis is based on the built-in knowledge graph. The skill has no external data access beyond its source repository URL.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports both JSON and Markdown output formats. You can also save output directly to a file using the --save parameter.
